name: Absolutely Deep Dark Chocolate Fudge Biscuits
recipeIngredient: 8 oz Plain Chocolate, broken into 16 pieces
4 oz Unsweetened Chocolate, broken into 8 pieces
recipeInstructions:
[Note: Instead of the unsweetened chocolate, I just used an extra 4oz of plain chocolate and got perfect results. I think as long as you're using really good plain chocolate, say at least 55% cocoa solids, it doesn't really matter!]
Preheat oven to 325°F / 170°C.
Sift together the flour, cocoa powder, bicarbonate of soda, and salt. Set aside.
Heat 1 inch of water in the bottom half of a double boiler over medium heat. Place plain and unsweetened chocolates in the top half of the double boiler. Tightly cover with cling film and allow to heat for 12 to 15 minutes. Remove from the heat and stir until smooth. Keep at room temperature until needed.
In the bowl of an electric mixer fitted with a paddle, beat together the butter and light brown sugar on medium speed for 1 minute. Scrape down the sides of the bowl and continue beating on high for an additional 30 seconds. Scrape down the bowl.
Add eggs one at a time while continuing to beat on medium, stopping to scrape the bowl after each addition. Add vanilla essence and beat on medium for 30 seconds. Add melted chocolate and beat on low for 10 more seconds. Scrape down the bowl and beat until smooth, about another 30 seconds.
Add sifted flour, cocoa powder, bicarbonate of soda, salt, and chocolate chips to the mixer in batches. Mix on a low setting until thoroughly combined, about 20-30 seconds. Remove from mixer and stir with a rubber spatula until well mixed.
Portion dough onto non-stick baking sheets using 2 level tablespoons per biscuit. Place biscuits in the top and middle oven racks and bake for 18 to 22 minutes, rotating trays halfway through. Allow to cool on baking sheets for 5-6 minutes before transferring to a cooling rack to cool completely. Store cooled biscuits in a sealed container.
Makes approximately 3 dozen biscuits
From Marcel Desaulniers' "Death By Chocolate"
